creator: ddavid@ican.net
text:
After upgrading to XFree86-4-4.0.2_2, Makefile=1.106 ( for AA support )
and also QT-2.2.3 Makefile=1.69, which both built and installed just fine, and trying to re-build kde starting with
kdesupport2, I receive the following errors resulting in kde2 not compiling.
(by hand) checking for Qt... configure: error:  Qt (>= 2.2.1) (libraries) not found. Please check your installation.
als0 in the kdesupport2 config.log, there is mention of the same problem reported by Jeremy  Shaffner (Dec27) Threaded X and KDE.
I noticed the commit for QT Makefile 1.68 was "untested" would this have something to do with it by chance?
